title: Kemulariella rosea
taxonomy: Kemulariella rosea is classified as a species of fungus in the genus Kemulariella. This genus belongs to the family Pleosporaceae, which is a group of fungi commonly known as coelomycetous anamorphs. The family Pleosporaceae is part of the order Pleosporales, which is a large and diverse group of fungi. The order Pleosporales is part of the class Dothideomycetes, which is a class of fungi that are primarily saprotrophic and parasitic. This classification places Kemulariella rosea within the kingdom Fungi.

It is reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in treating various health issues.
